rules.limit: Verification required via GSFA Online Database (Codex STAN 192-1995)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) sets forth the conditions for permitted food additives in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the database and its Preamble for specific provisions.
rules.evidenceQuote: The 'Codex General Standard for Food Additives' (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods

rules.limit: Verification required via General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
rules.notes: Codex establishes maximum levels (MLs) and guideline levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants in food and feed. These levels are legally permitted concentrations.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.limit: Follow Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices
rules.notes: Specific code of practice for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxin contamination in spices.
rules.evidenceQuote: CXC 78-2017 Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices

rules.notes: The EU Additives Database, based on Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008, provides information on approved food additives and their conditions of use in the EU.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites in specific foods, and process hygiene criteria.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.parameter: Permitted Food Additives and their Maximum Levels
rules.notes: Food business operators must refer to the full compendium for specific additive permissions and maximum levels for their products.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Food_Additives_Regulations

rules.notes: Food business operators must refer to the full compendium for specific allergen declaration requirements.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Labelling_Display

rules.notes: Food business operators must refer to the full compendium for all general labelling and display requirements.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Labelling_Display

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare (MHLW)

Class II chemical preservatives can only be directly added to fillings and toppings in kuehs, within regulatory limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: Class II chemical preservatives can only be directly added, within the prescribed regulatory limits, to fillings and toppings in kuehs.

rules.parameter: Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
rules.notes: Class II chemical preservatives should not be directly added during the manufacturing of kueh.
rules.evidenceQuote: Class II chemical preservatives, such as sorbic acid and benzoic acid, should not be directly added when manufacturing the kueh.

rules.notes: Specific limits for food additives vary by additive and food product type. Refer to SFA's Guidance Information.
rules.evidenceQuote: Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products.

rules.notes: Food additives must undergo safety assessment by SFA before use in food sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.notes: When using two or more Class II chemical preservatives, the SFA's Calculation Tool must be used to determine permitted levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food... use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels.

rules.notes: Businesses must use the SFA's List of Permitted Food Additives or Food Additives Search tool to verify if additives are allowed.
rules.evidenceQuote: Download the List of Permitted Food Additives or use the Food Additives Search tool to check if your additives are allowed.

rules.notes: Import or sale of food products containing unpermitted additives is prohibited.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: All food businesses must comply with regulatory limits on contaminants.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents i.e. contaminants in food.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Contaminants in food sold in Singapore must comply with SFA's regulatory limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Import or sale of food products containing contaminants exceeding regulatory limits is prohibited.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: SFA has established Maximum Residue Limits (MRLs) for other veterinary drug residues, available for download from the SFA website.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.parameter: Various contaminants (microbiological, heavy metals, mycotoxins, marine biotoxins, veterinary drug residues, pesticide residues, other incidental constituents)
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Specific limits for different types of contaminants are available in SFA's relevant documents.
rules.evidenceQuote: There are various limits imposed on different types of contaminants or incidental constituents in food. For more details, please refer to the relevant documents below: Microbiological standards, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, Other incidental constituents.

rules.foodCategory: Meat and food derived from meat
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Import or sale of meat and food derived from meat containing these specific veterinary drug residues is prohibited under Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of the following compounds: diethylstibestrol... hexoestrol... dienoestrol

rules.notes: All ingredients and additives used in prepacked food products must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refer to SFA's documents for specific microbiological standards.
rules.evidenceQuote: Microbiological standards

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Ready-to-eat food products are not required to be tested for Total Plate Count (TPC).
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Food and Drug Safety (MFDS)

Regulations for permitted and non-permitted food additives and their usage limits are established by the Thai FDA.
rules.evidenceQuote: วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร; ส่วนประกอบที่อนุญาต/ไม่อนุญาตให้ใช้ในอาหาร

rules.notes: Food standards for contaminants are established by the Thai FDA.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารปนเปื้อน

rules.limit: Prohibited from production, import, or sale
rules.notes: Certain food products are explicitly prohibited from production, import, or sale in Thailand. Verification is required to ensure the product is not on this list.
rules.evidenceQuote: อาหารที่ห้ามผลิต นำเข้า หรือจำหน่าย

rules.parameter: Toxic Substances and Veterinary Drug Residues
rules.notes: Food standards for toxic substances and veterinary drug residues are established by the Thai FDA. Draft ASEAN MRLs are also under consideration.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารพิษ-ยาสัตว์ตกค้าง; (ร่าง) ค่าปริมาณสารพิษตกค้างสูงสุดของอาเซียน (ASEAN MRLs)

rules.notes: Food products must comply with general food labeling and nutrition labeling requirements.
rules.evidenceQuote: การแสดงฉลากอาหารและฉลากโภชนาการ

rules.notes: Food standards for pathogenic microorganisms are established by the Thai FDA.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารด้านจุลินทรีย์ที่ทำให้เกิดโรค
